# rez-next Full LLMs Reference

> Complete API reference for AI agents. For concise index, see llms.txt.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Project Overview](#project-overview)
2. [Installation & Setup](#installation--setup)

5. [CLI Commands](#cli-commands)

7. [Development Guide](#development-guide)
8. [Testing](#testing)
9. [Benchmarks](#benchmarks)
10. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## Project Overview

**rez-next** is a Rust rewrite of the [Rez](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/rez) package manager, providing:

- **Performance**: 10-100x faster than Python Rez (see benchmarks)
- **Safety**: Memory-safe Rust, type-checked Python bindings
- **Compatibility**: Drop-in `import rez_next as rez` for most workflows
- **Modern tooling**: Cargo, PyO3, Maturin, Criterion

### Component Status

| Component | Status | Test Count |
|-----------|--------|------------|
| `rez-next-version` | Mature | ~30 |
| `rez-next-package` | Mature | ~25 |
| `rez-next-common` | Mature | ~10 |
| `rez-next-rex` | Mature | ~20 |
| `rez-next-solver` | Active dev | ~15 |
| `rez-next-context` | Active dev | ~12 |
| `rez-next-repository` | Mature | ~8 |
| `rez-next-build` | Partial | ~6 |
| `rez-next-cache` | Active dev | ~5 |
| `rez-next-suites` | Active dev | ~10 |
| `rez-next-bind` | Active dev | ~37 |
| `rez-next-search` | Active dev | ~16 |
| `rez-next-python` | Partial (18 submodules) | ~125 |

---

## Installation & Setup

### From Source (Rust Binary)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/loonghao/rez-next
cd rez-next
cargo build --release
./target/release/rez-next --version
```

### Python Bindings (PyPI)

```bash
pip install rez-next
```

### Python Bindings (Development)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/loonghao/rez-next
cd rez-next
maturin develop --release
pytest
```

### Prerequisites

- Rust 1.70+
- Python 3.8+ (for bindings)
- [just](https://github.com/casey/just) (optional, for `just` commands)
- [vx](https://github.com/loonghao/vx) (recommended, for tool management)

---

## Core Python API

### Version Operations (`rez_next.version`)

```python
from rez_next.version import PyVersion, PyVersionRange

# Parse versions
v1 = PyVersion("1.2.3-alpha.1")
v2 = PyVersion("2.0.0")
print(v1 < v2)  # True

# Version ranges
r = PyVersionRange(">=3.9,<4.0")
print(r.contains(v1))  # False for v1="1.2.3"

# Rez-compatible semantics
v3 = PyVersion("1.0")
v4 = PyVersion("1.0.0")
print(v3 > v4)  # True (Rez semantic: "1.0" > "1.0.0")
```

### Package Queries (`rez_next.packages_`)

```python
from rez_next.packages_ import (
    get_latest_package,
    iter_packages,
    get_package,
    copy_package,
    move_package,
    remove_package
)

# Get latest version
pkg = get_latest_package("python")
print(pkg.name, pkg.version)

# Iterate all versions
for p in iter_packages("maya", range_=">=2023"):
    print(p.version)

# Get specific version
pkg = get_package("python", "3.9.7")

# Copy/move/remove
copy_package(pkg, "/new/path")
move_package(pkg, "/new/path")
remove_package(pkg)
```

### Dependency Resolution (`rez_next.resolved_context`)

```python
from rez_next.resolved_context import ResolvedContext, resolve_packages

# Resolve packages
ctx = resolve_packages(["python-3.9", "maya-2024", "numpy-1.24"])
print(ctx.status)  # "solved"
print(ctx.resolved_packages)

# Load/Save context
ctx.save(".rez/context.rxt")
ctx2 = ResolvedContext.load(".rez/context.rxt")

# Get environment
env = ctx.get_environment()
```

### Context Diff (`rez_next.diff`)

```python
from rez_next.diff import diff_contexts, format_diff

result = diff_contexts(
    ["python-3.9", "maya-2023"],
    ["python-3.11", "maya-2024", "houdini-20"]
)

print(f"Added: {result.num_added}")
print(f"Removed: {result.num_removed}")
print(f"Upgraded: {result.num_upgraded}")
print(format_diff(result))
```

### Reverse Dependencies (`rez_next.depends`)

```python
from rez_next.depends import get_reverse_dependencies, print_depends

# Find what depends on python
result = get_reverse_dependencies("python", transitive=True)
print(result.format())

# Output:
#   Reverse dependencies for 'python':
#     Direct:
#       maya-2024.1  (requires 'python-3.9')
#     Transitive:
#       nuke-14.0  (requires 'maya-2024')
```

### Active Context Status (`rez_next.status`)

```python
from rez_next.status import get_current_status, is_in_rez_context

if is_in_rez_context():
    status = get_current_status()
    print(f"Active packages: {status.resolved_packages}")
    print(f"Shell: {status.current_shell}")
```

### Package Search (`rez_next.search`)

```python
from rez_next.search import search_packages, search_package_names

# Search packages
results = search_packages("maya")
for r in results:
    print(r.name, r.version)

# Regex search
names = search_package_names("^py")  # matches "python", "pyqt5", etc.
```

### Shell Completion (`rez_next.complete`)

```python
from rez_next.complete import get_completion_script

# Generate bash completion
bash_script = get_completion_script("bash")
print(bash_script)

# Generate zsh completion
zsh_script = get_completion_script("zsh")
```

### Suite Management (`rez_next.suite`)

```python
from rez_next.suite import Suite, SuiteTool

# Create suite
suite = Suite("my_suite")
suite.add_tool(SuiteTool("maya", "2024"))
suite.add_tool(SuiteTool("python", "3.9"))
suite.save("suite.yaml")

# Load suite
suite2 = Suite.load("suite.yaml")
```

### Configuration (`rez_next.config`)

```python
from rez_next.config import get_config

config = get_config()
print(config.packages_path)
print(config.local_packages_path)
```

### System Info (`rez_next.system`)

```python
from rez_next.system import System

sys = System()
print(sys.platform)  # "linux", "windows", "darwin"
print(sys.arch)      # "x86_64", "arm64"
print(sys.python_version)
```

### Rex Commands (`rez_next.rex`)

```python
from rez_next.rex import RexExecutor

rex = RexExecutor()
rex.setenv("MY_VAR", "value")
rex.prepend_path("/usr/local/bin")
rex.alias("ll", "ls -lh")
script = rex.generate_shell_script("bash")
```

---

## Rust Crate API

### rez-next-version

```rust
use rez_next_version::{Version, VersionRange};

let v1: Version = "1.2.3-alpha.1".parse().unwrap();
let v2: Version = "2.0.0".parse().unwrap();
assert!(v1 < v2);

let range: VersionRange = ">=3.9,<4.0".parse().unwrap();
assert!(range.contains(&v1));
```

### rez-next-package

```rust
use rez_next_package::{Package, PackageParser};

let parser = PackageParser::new();
let pkg = parser.parse_file("package.py")?;
println!("Name: {}", pkg.name);
println!("Version: {}", pkg.version);
```

### rez-next-solver

```rust
use rez_next_solver::{Solver, SolverRequest};

let request = SolverRequest::new()
    .add_requirement("python-3.9")
    .add_requirement("maya-2024");

let solver = Solver::new();
let result = solver.solve(request)?;
```

### rez-next-repository

```rust
use rez_next_repository::{Repository, RepositoryScanner};

let repo = Repository::from_path("/packages");
let pkgs = repo.find_packages("python")?;
```

---

## CLI Commands

### Self-Update

```bash
rez-next self-update              # Update to latest
rez-next self-update --check      # Check for updates
rez-next self-update --version 0.3.0  # Specific version
rez-next self-update --force      # Force reinstall
```

### Package Operations

```bash
rez-next search "maya"            # Search packages
rez-next search --regex "^py"    # Regex search
rez-next depends python           # Reverse dependencies
rez-next diff ctx1.rxt ctx2.rxt  # Diff contexts
rez-next status                   # Show active context
```

### Build & Release

```bash
rez-next build .                  # Build package
rez-next release .                # Release package
rez-next bind python              # Bind system tool
```

---

## Configuration

### rezconfig.py

rez-next reads the same `~/.rez/rezconfig.py` as Rez.

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description | Default |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `REZ_PACKAGES_PATH` | Package search path | `~/.rez/packages` |
| `REZ_LOCAL_PACKAGES_PATH` | Local package path | `./packages` |
| `RUST_LOG` | Rust logging level | `info` |

---

## Development Guide

### Build Commands (using vx + just)

```bash
vx just build           # Dev build
vx just build-release   # Release build
vx just test            # Run all tests
vx just lint            # Clippy linting
vx just fmt             # Format code
vx just ci              # Full CI check
vx just bench           # Run benchmarks
vx just doc-check       # Check documentation
```

### Project Structure

```
rez-next/
├── crates/                    # Rust crates
│   ├── rez-next-common/
│   ├── rez-next-version/
│   ├── rez-next-package/
│   ├── rez-next-solver/
│   ├── rez-next-repository/
│   ├── rez-next-context/
│   ├── rez-next-build/
│   ├── rez-next-cache/
│   ├── rez-next-rex/
│   ├── rez-next-suites/
│   ├── rez-next-bind/
│   ├── rez-next-search/
│   └── rez-next-python/      # Python bindings
├── src/                       # CLI binary
├── tests/                     # Integration tests
├── benches/                   # Benchmarks
├── docs/                      # Documentation
└── metrics/                   # Performance tracking
```

### Adding a New Feature

1. **Choose the right crate** (see Architecture in AGENTS.md)
2. **Implement in Rust** with tests
3. **Expose via PyO3** in `rez-next-python`
4. **Add Python tests** in `tests/`
5. **Update documentation**
6. **Run full CI**: `vx just ci`

### Code Style

- Rust: `cargo fmt` + Clippy clean
- Python: Ruff (configured in repo)
- Commits: Conventional Commits (enforced by release-please)

---

## Testing

### Run All Tests

```bash
vx just test
# Equivalent to:
vx cargo test --workspace --exclude rez-next-python
```

### Run Specific Tests

```bash
# Rust tests
vx cargo test --package rez-next-version

# Python tests
maturin develop --release
pytest tests/
```

### Test Coverage

```bash
cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lcov --output-path coverage.lcov
```

### CI Tests

CI runs on Linux/macOS/Windows:
- `vx just fmt-check`
- `vx just lint-ci`
- `vx just doc-check`
- `vx cargo test --workspace --exclude rez-next-python`
- `vx just cli-e2e`
- `cargo llvm-cov`
- Python binding tests (maturin + pytest)

---

## Benchmarks

### Run Benchmarks

```bash
vx just bench
# Equivalent to:
cargo bench --workspace
```

### Benchmark Results

| Operation | Time |
|-----------|------|
| Parse version | ~9.1 us |
| Compare versions | ~6.8 ns |
| Sort 1000 versions | ~176 us |
| Create package | ~35 ns |
| Serialize to YAML | ~7.0 us |
| Serialize to JSON | ~3.4 us |

### Python API Performance

| Operation | Time (avg) | Throughput |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| `pip_install()` | ~420 ns | 2.38M ops/sec |
| `walk_packages()` | ~42 μs | 23.9K ops/sec |

---

## Troubleshooting

### Build Fails

```bash
# Clean and rebuild
cargo clean
vx just build
```

### Python Bindings Not Working

```bash
# Reinstall maturin
pip install maturin --upgrade
maturin develop --release
```

### Clippy Errors

```bash
vx cargo clippy --fix --workspace --all-targets --all-features
```

### Dependency Issues

```bash
cargo update
cargo audit --update-db
cargo deny check
```

---

## Advanced Topics

### Custom Solver Configuration

```rust
use rez_next_solver::{Solver, SolverConfig};

let config = SolverConfig::default()
    .with_max_iterations(1000)
    .with_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30));

let solver = Solver::with_config(config);
```

### Cache Configuration

```rust
use rez_next_cache::{Cache, CacheConfig};

let cache = Cache::with_config(
    CacheConfig::default()
        .with_memory_size(1000)
        .with_disk_path("/tmp/rez-cache")
);
```

### Plugin System

```python
from rez_next.plugins import PluginManager

pm = PluginManager()
pm.load_plugin("my_plugin")
```

---

## Migration from Rez

### Drop-in Replacement

```python
# Before (Rez)
import rez
from rez.packages_ import iter_packages

# After (rez-next)
import rez_next as rez
from rez_next.packages_ import iter_packages
```

### Compatibility Checklist

- [x] Version parsing
- [x] Package queries
- [x] Dependency resolution
- [x] Context management
- [x] Suite management
- [x] Shell integration
- [x] Rex commands
- [ ] Full build system (partial)
- [ ] Full release workflow (partial)
- [ ] All CLI commands (growing)

---

**For AI Agents**: This document is comprehensive. Start with `llms.txt` for quick reference, use this document for detailed API information. Always check `docs/python-integration.md` for current Python coverage before suggesting implementations.
